question: Which cloud model gives you virtual servers, storage, and networking?
note: IaaS gives you infrastructure; you manage the OS and above.

question: Which cloud model lets you deploy your code while the provider manages the platform?
note: PaaS is a managed platform for your app/code.

question: Which cloud model is Microsoft 365 an example of?
note: Microsoft 365 is software delivered as a service.

question: Which cloud type combines on-premises resources with cloud resources?
note: Hybrid cloud mixes on-premises and cloud resources.

question: Which cloud model provides a complete desktop environment?
note: Desktop as a Service.

question: Which cloud type is owned and operated by a third-party provider?
note: Public cloud is provider-owned.

question: Which cloud type is dedicated to one organization?
note: Private cloud is organization-owned.

question: Who manages the operating system in SaaS?
note: The provider manages everything in SaaS.

question: Who manages applications in IaaS?
note: You manage OS and applications in IaaS.

question: Which cloud model gives developers a platform but not server management?
note: PaaS abstracts infrastructure.

question: Which cloud model provides a complete desktop environment?
note: Desktop as a Service provides cloud-hosted desktops.

question: Which cloud type is owned and operated by a third-party provider?
note: Public clouds are available to many customers.

question: Which cloud type is dedicated to a single organization?
note: Private clouds are used by one organization.

question: Who manages the operating system in SaaS?
note: In SaaS the provider manages everything.

question: Who manages the operating system in IaaS?
note: In IaaS you manage the OS and applications.

question: Who manages applications in PaaS?
note: You provide the code and applications in PaaS.

question: Which cloud model is best described as 'renting virtual servers'?
note: IaaS provides virtual machines, storage, and networking.

question: Which cloud model is best described as 'bring your code'?
note: PaaS provides the platform while you provide the application.

question: Which cloud model is best described as 'just use the software'?
note: SaaS users simply consume the application.

question: Microsoft Azure virtual machines are an example of which cloud model?
note: Azure VMs provide infrastructure.

question: Microsoft Azure App Service is an example of which cloud model?
note: You deploy code while Microsoft manages the platform.

question: Which cloud model allows users to access a Windows desktop through the Internet?
note: DaaS delivers a full virtual desktop.

question: A company keeps Active Directory on-premises but uses Microsoft 365 for email. Which cloud type is this?
note: Part on-premises and part cloud equals hybrid.

question: A company runs all resources in its own datacenter and does not share them with others. Which cloud type is this?
note: Private cloud resources belong to one organization.

question: A company uses only AWS resources and has no on-premises infrastructure. Which cloud type is this?
note: All resources are hosted by a cloud provider.

question: Which cloud model requires the LEAST management from the customer?
note: The provider manages nearly everything.

question: Which cloud model requires the MOST management from the customer?
note: You manage the operating system, applications, and data.

question: Which cloud model would most closely resemble moving your Proxmox VMs into Azure virtual machines?
note: You are still managing the virtual machines.

question: Which cloud model would most closely resemble hosting a website where you only upload code?
note: The provider manages the underlying platform.

question: Which cloud model would Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, and Salesforce all be examples of?
note: These are software applications delivered over the Internet.
